title: Java Cheatsheet - Complete Reference
description: Java ko master karo basics se advanced tak - Swiggy-style food delivery example ke saath

concepts.name: Your First Java Program
concepts.explanation: Har Java program se shuru hota hai a class and a main method. Samjho the class as a Restaurant banana, and main() as the front entrance where customers (Java) enter to start their food ordering journey.
keyPoints: public = koi bhi access kar sakta hai (jaise public restaurant)
static = class ka hai, kisi specific object ka nahi
void = method kuch return nahi karta
main = Java's entry point - yahan se execution start hoti hai
String[] args = command-line arguments program ke liye
Har Java file mein ek class honi chahiye same name ke saath
Compilation: javac SwiggyApp.java → banta hai SwiggyApp.class
Execution: java SwiggyApp
Interview: Main static kyun hai? So JVM can call it without create karna object

concepts.name: Primitive Data Types
concepts.explanation: Java's banana blocks for storing simple values. Swiggy app mein, hum use karte hain different types store karne ke liye order quantities, prices, ratings, and status flags.
concepts.code:
// Whole numbers - for quantities and counts
byte smallQty = 5;              // 8-bit: -128 to 127
short mediumQty = 500;          // 16-bit: -32,768 to 32,767
int orderCount = 1_250_000;     // 32-bit: -2B to 2B (most common)
long totalRevenue = 45_000_000_000L;  // 64-bit (needs L suffix)

// Decimal numbers - for prices and ratings
float deliveryFee = 29.99f;     // 32-bit (needs f suffix)
double itemPrice = 349.50;      // 64-bit (default for decimals)
double rating = 4.5;            // Restaurant rating

// Other types
boolean isAvailable = true;     // true or false only
boolean isPremium = false;      // Is customer premium member?
char orderStatus = 'P';         // Single character (16-bit Unicode)
keyPoints: int default hai whole numbers ke liye (4 bytes, -2B to 2B)
double default hai decimals ke liye (8 bytes, high precision)
long chahiye 'L' suffix: 1000000L, float chahiye 'f': 3.14f
boolean yes/no flags ke liye (true/false sirf, not 0/1)
char single quotes use karta hai: 'A', String uses double: "Hello"
Readability ke liye underscores use karo: 1_000_000
byte/short rarely use hote hain - int is preferred hai integers ke liye
Default values: int=0, double=0.0, boolean=false, char='\u0000'
Interview: kya farak float and double? Precision (32-bit vs 64-bit)

concepts.name: Variables and Constants
concepts.explanation: Variables wo data store karte hain jo badal sakta hai (like order status), jabki constants fixed values store karte hain (like GST rate, platform fee).
keyPoints: Variables declare karte hain: dataType variableName = value;
Constants 'final' keyword use karte hain - reassign nahi kar sakte
Constant names UPPER_SNAKE_CASE by convention
Variables camelCase: orderTotal, customerName
Constants un values ke liye use karo jo kabhi nahi badlenge (rates, limits)
final reassignment prevent karta hai but objects ko immutable nahi banata doesn't make objects immutable
Local variables ko use se pehle initialize karna padta hai
Interview: Kya final variable change kar sakte ho? Nahi, compilation error aayega

concepts.name: Type Casting
concepts.explanation: Ek data type ko dusre mein convert karna. Jaise convert karna total bill (double) to rounded rupees (int), or expanding small quantity (byte) to bada count (int).
keyPoints: Implicit casting: chhota → bada type (automatic, safe)
Explicit casting: bada → chhota type (manual, data loss ho sakta hai)
Explicit ke liye syntax: (targetType) value
Widening: byte→short→int→long→float→double (automatic)
Narrowing: ulti direction mein explicit cast chahiye
int ko double mein cast karne se integer division prevent hota hai
Dhyan raho: large long ko int mein cast karne se overflow ho sakta hai
Interview: kya farak implicit and explicit casting?

concepts.name: Arithmetic Operators
concepts.explanation: Basic math operations calculate karne ke liye order totals, discounts, delivery fees, and splitting bills.
keyPoints: + addition, - subtraction, * multiplication
/ division (int/int se int milta hai, decimals ke liye double use karo ke liye)
% modulo (remainder - even/odd check ke liye useful)
++ increment by 1, -- decrement by 1
Order of operations: *, /, % pehle +, -
Order control karne ke liye parentheses use karo: (a + b) * c
Interview: mein kya farak? i++ and ++i? Post vs pre-increment
i++ purani value return karta hai phir increment, ++i pehle increment phir return

concepts.name: Comparison Operators
concepts.explanation: Values compare karne ke liye decisions lena - check karo ki order qualifies for free delivery, if restaurant is highly rated, if customer is eligible for discounts.
keyPoints: == equality check karta hai, != inequality check karta hai
> greater than, < less than
>= greater than or equal, <= less than or equal
Boolean return karta hai (true ya false)
Strings ke liye .equals() not ==
Example: "Biryani".equals("Biryani") → true
Interview: Strings ke liye == kyun nahi? References compare karta hai, content nahi

concepts.name: Logical Operators
concepts.explanation: Multiple conditions combine karna - check karo ki customer is premium AND order is above threshold, or if restaurant is open OR has late-night delivery.
keyPoints: && (AND): true sirf if BOTH conditions are true
|| (OR): tab true jab KAM SE KAM EK condition true ho
! (NOT): boolean value invert karta hai (true → false, false → true)
Short-circuit: && pehli false pe ruk jata hai, || pehli true pe
Clarity ke liye parentheses use karo: (a && b) || c
Interview: Short-circuit evaluation kya hai? Result determine hote hi evaluation ruk jati hai

concepts.name: Ternary Operator
concepts.explanation: Ek line mein compact if-else - jaldi se assign karo delivery fee ke basis pe order value, or set order priority ke basis pe customer type.
keyPoints: Syntax: condition ? valueIfTrue : valueIfFalse
Value return karta hai (variable ko assign kar sakte ho)
Simple assignments ke liye if-else se zyada concise
Nested ternary avoid karo - readability kam ho jati hai
Simple true/false decisions ke liye perfect
Dono value expressions same type ke hone chahiye
Interview: Ternary kab use karein? Simple conditional assignments sirf

concepts.name: if-else Statements
concepts.explanation: Decisions lena ke basis pe conditions - discounts apply karo, check delivery eligibility, orders validate karo.
keyPoints: if executes block sirf if condition is true
else tab execute hota hai jab if condition false ho
else-if multiple conditions ke liye (order mein check hote hain)
Curly braces {} single statements ke liye bhi use karo (best practice)
Conditions boolean evaluate honi chahiye
First true condition executes, rest are skip karoped
Interview: Agar koi condition true nahi hui? Kuch execute nahi hota (jab tak else na ho)

concepts.name: switch Statement
concepts.explanation: Multiple options mein se choose karo ke basis pe a value - handle karo order status, payment methods, delivery zones.
keyPoints: switch value ko multiple cases ke saath compare karta hai
break next case mein fall-through prevent karta hai
default execute hota hai agar koi case match nahi (optional but recommended)
Kaam karta hai: byte, short, int, char, String, enum
Kaam nahi karta: long, float, double, boolean
Cases compile-time constants hone chahiye
Interview: break ke bina kya hota hai? Next case mein fall-through ho jata hai
Java 12+: switch expressions -> (break ki zarurat nahi)

concepts.name: for Loop
concepts.explanation: Code ko specific number of times repeat karo - process karo menu items, totals calculate karo, display karo order history.
keyPoints: Syntax: for (initialization; condition; update) { }
initialization ek baar start mein run hota hai
condition checked pehle each iteration
update har iteration ke baad run hota hai
Tab use karo jab iterations ki sankhya pata ho
i++ sabse common update hai (increment by 1)
use karke i-- backwards count kar sakte ho
Interview: Loop variable ka scope kya hai? Inside loop sirf (agar for mein declare kiya)

concepts.name: Enhanced for Loop (for-each)
concepts.explanation: Collections ko aasani se iterate karo - process karo all menu items, customers, orders bina index management ke.
keyPoints: Syntax: for (Type variable : collection) { }
Regular for loop se simple
Index management ki zarurat nahi
Array elements ko directly modify nahi kar sakte
Backwards iterate nahi kar sakte
Index access nahi kar sakte (zarurat ho to regular for use karo)
Arrays aur collections ke saath kaam karta hai (ArrayList, etc.)
Interview: for-each kab use karein? When you need to process karo all elements sequentially

concepts.name: while Loop
concepts.explanation: Jab tak condition true hai tab tak repeat karo - wait karo order confirmation, process karo queue, failed operations retry karo.
keyPoints: Syntax: while (condition) { }
Condition checked pehle each iteration
Agar condition initially false hai to ek baar bhi execute nahi hoga
Tab use karo jab iterations ki sankhya pata nahi
Dhyan raho: infinite loop if condition never becomes false
Loop ke andar condition update karo infinite loop se bachne ke liye
Interview: mein kya farak? while and do-while? while pehle condition check karta hai

concepts.name: do-while Loop
concepts.explanation: Kam se kam ek baar execute karo, phir jab tak condition is true - menu kam se kam ek baar dikhao, input validate karo.
keyPoints: Syntax: do { } while (condition);
Executes body at least once pehle checking condition
Har iteration ke baad condition check hoti hai
Tab use karo jab kam se kam ek baar execute karna zaruri ho
Common use: input validation, menu systems
ke baad semicolon mat bhulna while(condition);
Interview: do-while kab use karein? Jab body kam se kam ek baar execute honi chahiye

concepts.name: break and continue
concepts.explanation: Loop flow control karo - jaldi exit karo jab order found, skip karo cancelled orders, error pe ruk jao.
keyPoints: break: loop se turant bahar nikal jao
continue: skip karos current iteration, moves to next
break for, while, do-while, switch
continue sirf for, while, do-while sirf
In nested loops, break/continue affects innermost loop sirf
Outer loops ke liye labeled break/continue use karo (rare)
Interview: mein kya farak? break and continue? break exits, continue skip karos

concepts.name: Array Basics
concepts.explanation: Fixed-size containers same type ki multiple values store karne ke liye - menu items, prices, customer ratings.
keyPoints: Fixed size - creation ke baad grow ya shrink nahi kar sakte
Zero-indexed: pehla element index 0 pe
Syntax: Type[] name = new Type[size];
Values ke saath initialize: Type[] name = {val1, val2, ...};
Access: array[index], Modify: array[index] = value
Length: array.length (property hai, method nahi)
ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException agar index < 0 or >= length
Default values: int=0, double=0.0, boolean=false, objects=null
Interview: Array vs ArrayList? Array fixed size, ArrayList dynamic

concepts.name: Multi-dimensional Arrays
concepts.explanation: Arrays ke arrays - restaurant menu with categories, delivery zones with areas, order history by month.
keyPoints: 2D array: Type[][] name = new Type[rows][cols];
Initialize: Type[][] name = {{row1}, {row2}, ...};
Access: array[row][column]
Jagged arrays allowed (alag alag length ki rows)
3D arrays possible: Type[][][] name
Interview: Length kaise nikalen? array.length for rows, array[i].length for columns

concepts.name: Array Utility Methods
concepts.explanation: Java provide karta hai Arrays class utility methods ke saath sorting, searching, comparing, and filling arrays.
keyPoints: Arrays.sort() ascending order mein sort karta hai (original modify hota hai)
Arrays.binarySearch() sorted array chahiye, index ya negative return karta hai
Arrays.fill() saare elements ko specified value set karta hai
Arrays.copyOf() banta hai new array with copied elements
Arrays.equals() content compare karta hai, references nahi
Arrays.toString() array ko readable string mein convert karta hai
Time complexity: sort O(n log n), binarySearch O(log n)
Interview: Descending order mein kaise sort karein? Use Collections.reverseOrder() wrapper types ke saath

concepts.name: String Basics
concepts.explanation: Strings immutable hain sequences hain characters ke - customer names, addresses, menu items, order IDs.
keyPoints: Strings immutable hain - creation ke baad change nahi ho sakte
String pool: literals special memory area mein store hote hain
Comparison ke liye .equals() use karo, == nahi
== references compare karta hai, .equals() content compare karta hai
charAt() StringIndexOutOfBoundsException throw karta hai agar invalid index
substring(start, end): end exclusive hai
Interview: Strings immutable kyun hain? Security, thread-safety, caching

concepts.name: String Methods
concepts.explanation: Common string operations validation, formatting, searching in customer data and menu items.
keyPoints: trim() leading aur trailing whitespace remove karta hai
isEmpty() checks if length is 0, isBlank() checks if sirf whitespace
contains() case-sensitive hai
replace() saari occurrences replace karta hai
split() regex pattern use karta hai
indexOf() -1 return karta hai agar nahi mila
Saare methods naya String return karte hain (immutability)
Interview: mein kya farak? isEmpty() and isBlank()? isBlank whitespace bhi check karta hai

concepts.name: StringBuilder
concepts.explanation: Mutable string efficient concatenation ke liye - banana order summaries, generate karna receipts, create karna dynamic messages.
keyPoints: StringBuilder mutable hai - can be modified
String concatenation se zyada efficient loop mein
Not thread-safe (use StringBuffer for thread-safety)
Initial capacity: 16 characters
Automatically grows when needed
append() is most common operation
toString() converts to String
Interview: String vs StringBuilder? String immutable, StringBuilder mutable and efficient for concatenation

concepts.name: Classes and Objects
concepts.explanation: Classes are blueprints, objects are instances. A Restaurant class defines what all restaurants have (name, rating, menu), and each restaurant object (McDonald's, KFC) has its own specific values.
keyPoints: Class = blueprint/template, Object = actual instance
Fields store object state (data)
Methods define object behavior (actions)
Constructor initializes object (same name as class, no return type)
this keyword use karte hain refers to current object
new keyword use karte hain banta hai object and calls constructor
Each object has its own copy of instance variables
Interview: What is 'this'? Reference to current object

concepts.name: Encapsulation
concepts.explanation: Hide internal details and provide controlled access - protect customer balance, validate order quantities, ensure data integrity.
keyPoints: private = accessible sirf within the class
public = accessible from anywhere
Getters provide read access: getFieldName()
Setters provide write access with validation: setFieldName()
Encapsulation protects data integrity
Prevents direct access to fields
Allows changing internal implementation without affecting users
Interview: Why encapsulation? Data hiding, validation, flexibility

concepts.name: Inheritance
concepts.explanation: Child class inherits properties and methods from parent class - DeliveryPartner and RestaurantOwner both inherit from User, avoiding code duplication.
keyPoints: extends keyword use karte hain establishes inheritance
Child inherits all non-private members from parent
super() calls parent constructor (must be first line)
super.method() calls parent method
protected = accessible in same package and subclasses
@Override annotation indicates method overriding
Java supports single inheritance sirf (one parent)
Use interfaces for multiple inheritance
Interview: Why single inheritance? Avoids diamond problem

concepts.name: Polymorphism - Method Overriding
concepts.explanation: Child class provides specific implementation of parent method - different order types calculate total differently.
keyPoints: Method overriding: child provides specific implementation
Method signature must match parent (name, parameters, return type)
@Override annotation recommended (compile-time check)
Runtime polymorphism: method called ke basis pe actual object type
Parent reference can hold child object: Order order = new PremiumOrder()
Overridden method called ke basis pe object, not reference type
Cannot override: final, static, private methods
Interview: Overriding vs Overloading? Overriding = same signature in child, Overloading = same name different parameters

concepts.name: Polymorphism - Method Overloading
concepts.explanation: Multiple methods with same name but different parameters - addItem() can add single item, multiple items, or item with quantity.
keyPoints: Method overloading: same name, different parameters
Compile-time polymorphism (decided at compile time)
Parameters must differ in: number, type, or order
Return type alone doesn't distinguish methods
Compiler chooses method ke basis pe arguments
Can overload constructors too
Improves code readability and flexibility
Interview: Can you overload main()? Yes, but JVM sirf calls main(String[] args)

concepts.name: Abstraction - Abstract Classes
concepts.explanation: Abstract classes provide partial implementation - Payment is abstract, specific payment methods (UPI, Card, Cash) implement the details.
keyPoints: abstract class cannot be instantiated
Can have abstract methods (no body) and concrete methods (with body)
Child must implement all abstract methods or be abstract itself
Can have constructors, fields, and concrete methods
Use when classes share common behavior but differ in implementation
0-100% abstraction possible
Can extend sirf one abstract class
Interview: Abstract class vs Interface? Abstract can have state and concrete methods

concepts.name: Abstraction - Interfaces
concepts.explanation: Interfaces define contracts - what a class must do, not how. Trackable interface ensures Order and DeliveryPartner can both be tracked.
keyPoints: Interface = 100% abstraction (pehle Java 8)
All methods public and abstract by default
All fields public, static, and final by default
Class can implement multiple interfaces
Use 'implements' keyword use karte hain
Must implement all interface methods
Java 8+: can have default and static methods
Java 9+: can have private methods
Interview: When to use interface? When unrelated classes need same behavior

concepts.name: Access Modifiers
concepts.explanation: Control visibility of classes, methods, and fields - protect sensitive data, expose public API.
keyPoints: public: accessible everywhere
private: accessible sirf within the class
protected: accessible in same package and subclasses
default (no modifier): accessible in same package sirf
Use private for fields, public for methods (generally)
Encapsulation: private fields + public getters/setters
Interview: Access modifier order? public > protected > default > private

concepts.name: try-catch-finally
concepts.explanation: Handle errors gracefully without crashing - handle karo payment failures, invalid orders, network issues.
keyPoints: try: code that might throw exception
catch: handle karo specific exception type
finally: always executes (cleanup code)
Multiple catch blocks: specific to general
finally executes even if return in try/catch
Use e.getMessage() for error message
Use e.printStackTrace() for full stack trace
Interview: When does finally not execute? System.exit() or JVM crash

concepts.name: throw and throws
concepts.explanation: Manually throw exceptions or declare that method might throw - validate order data, enforce business rules.
keyPoints: throw: actually throws an exception
throws: declares method might throw exception
throw used inside method body
throws used in method signature
Checked exceptions must be handle karod or declared
Unchecked exceptions (RuntimeException) don't need throws
Can throw multiple exception types: throws IOException, SQLException
Interview: Checked vs Unchecked? Checked must be handle karod, Unchecked are runtime errors

concepts.name: Custom Exceptions
concepts.explanation: Create domain-specific exceptions - InvalidOrderException, PaymentFailedException, RestaurantClosedException.
keyPoints: Extend Exception for checked exceptions
Extend RuntimeException for unchecked exceptions
Provide meaningful error messages
Can add custom fields and methods
Use for domain-specific errors
Makes code more readable and maintainable
Interview: When to create custom exception? When built-in exceptions don't convey business meaning

concepts.name: ArrayList
concepts.explanation: Dynamic array that grows/shrinks - store menu items, orders, customers. Most commsirf used collection.
keyPoints: Dynamic size - grows automatically
Backed by array internally
Initial capacity: 10, grows by 50% when full
Maintains insertion order
Allows duplicates and null
Fast random access: O(1)
Slow insertion/deletion in middle: O(n)
Not thread-safe (use Collections.synchronizedList() or CopyOnWriteArrayList)
Interview: ArrayList vs Array? ArrayList dynamic, Array fixed size

concepts.name: LinkedList
concepts.explanation: Doubly-linked list - efficient insertion/deletion at ends. Good for queue of pending orders, delivery partner assignments.
keyPoints: Doubly-linked list implementation
Efficient insertion/deletion at ends: O(1)
Slow random access: O(n)
More memory per element (stores prev/next references)
Can be used as Stack, Queue, or Deque
Maintains insertion order
Allows duplicates and null
Not thread-safe
Interview: ArrayList vs LinkedList? ArrayList for random access, LinkedList for frequent insertions/deletions

concepts.name: HashMap
concepts.explanation: Key-value pairs for fast lookup - restaurant ID to Restaurant object, customer ID to Customer, order ID to Order.
keyPoints: Stores key-value pairs
Keys must be unique (duplicate key overwrites value)
Fast operations: O(1) average for get/put/remove
Initial capacity: 16, load factor: 0.75
Grows when size > capacity * load factor
No guaranteed order
Allows one null key and multiple null values
Not thread-safe (use ConcurrentHashMap)
Interview: How HashMap works? Uses hashing, stores in buckets, handle karos collisions with linked list/tree

concepts.name: HashSet
concepts.explanation: Unique elements sirf - store unique customer IDs, restaurant tags, order statuses. No duplicates allowed.
keyPoints: No duplicate elements
Backed by HashMap internally
Fast operations: O(1) average for add/remove/contains
No guaranteed order
Allows one null element
Not thread-safe
Use when uniqueness is required
Interview: HashSet vs TreeSet? HashSet faster but unordered, TreeSet sorted but slower

concepts.name: TreeSet
concepts.explanation: Sorted set - automatically sorts elements. Store restaurants by rating, orders by time, menu items alphabetically.
keyPoints: Sorted set - maintains elements in sorted order
Backed by TreeMap (Red-Black tree)
Operations: O(log n) for add/remove/contains
No duplicates
Does not allow null (throws NullPointerException)
Elements must be Comparable or provide Comparator
Use when sorted order is required
Interview: TreeSet vs HashSet? TreeSet sorted O(log n), HashSet unsorted O(1)

concepts.name: LinkedHashMap
concepts.explanation: HashMap with insertion order - track order of menu items added, maintain customer visit history.
keyPoints: Maintains insertion order (or access order)
Slightly slower than HashMap due to linked list overhead
Operations: O(1) for get/put/remove
Allows one null key and multiple null values
Useful for LRU cache implementation
Not thread-safe
Interview: HashMap vs LinkedHashMap? LinkedHashMap maintains order, HashMap doesn't

concepts.name: TreeMap
concepts.explanation: Sorted map - automatically sorts by keys. Store restaurants by rating, menu items by price, orders by timestamp.
keyPoints: Sorted map - maintains keys in sorted order
Backed by Red-Black tree
Operations: O(log n) for get/put/remove
Does not allow null keys (throws NullPointerException)
Allows null values
Keys must be Comparable or provide Comparator
Use when sorted key order is required
Interview: TreeMap vs HashMap? TreeMap sorted O(log n), HashMap unsorted O(1)

concepts.name: PriorityQueue
concepts.explanation: Queue with priority - process karo high-priority orders first, assign urgent deliveries, handle karo VIP customers.
keyPoints: Priority queue - elements ordered by priority
Backed by binary heap
Operations: O(log n) for add/poll, O(1) for peek
Default: min heap (smallest/highest priority first)
Elements must be Comparable or provide Comparator
Does not allow null
Not thread-safe
Interview: When to use PriorityQueue? When you need to process karo elements by priority, not insertion order

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.explanation: LIFO (Last In First Out) - undo operations, navigation history, expression evaluation.
keyPoints: LIFO: Last In First Out
Extends Vector (legacy class)
push() adds to top, pop() removes from top
peek() views top without removing
empty() checks if stack is empty
Thread-safe (synchronized methods)
Consider using Deque instead (more efficient)
Interview: Stack vs Queue? Stack LIFO, Queue FIFO

concepts.name: Queue and Deque
concepts.explanation: FIFO queue and double-ended queue - order process karoing queue, delivery partner assignment, task scheduling.
keyPoints: Queue: FIFO (First In First Out)
offer() adds, poll() removes, peek() views
Deque: can add/remove from both ends
ArrayDeque more efficient than LinkedList for queue/stack
ArrayDeque doesn't allow null
Not thread-safe (use ConcurrentLinkedQueue)
Interview: When to use Deque? When you need to add/remove from both ends

concepts.name: Generic Classes
concepts.explanation: Type-safe classes that work with any type - create reusable Order container, Response wrapper, Cache implementation.
keyPoints: Generics provide type safety at compile time
Eliminates need for casting
Type parameter naming: T (Type), E (Element), K (Key), V (Value)
Can have multiple type parameters: <K, V>
Type erasure: generic type info removed at runtime
Cannot create generic array: new T[10] is invalid
Interview: Why generics? Type safety, code reusability, eliminates casting

concepts.name: Generic Methods
concepts.explanation: Methods that work with different types - utility methods for printing, comparing, converting data.
keyPoints: Generic methods can be in non-generic classes
Type parameter declared pehle return type: <T> void method()
Bounded type parameters: <T extends Number>
Multiple bounds: <T extends Class & Interface>
Type inference: compiler determines type from arguments
Interview: Generic method vs Generic class? Method has type parameter in method signature

concepts.name: Wildcards
concepts.explanation: Flexible generic types - accept any type of list, work with unknown types, create flexible APIs.
keyPoints: ? = wildcard (unknown type)
? extends T = upper bounded (T or subclasses)
? super T = lower bounded (T or superclasses)
Use extends for reading, super for writing
PECS: Producer Extends, Consumer Super
Cannot add elements to ? extends (except null)
Interview: When to use wildcards? When you don't care about specific type

concepts.name: Lambda Expressions
concepts.explanation: Concise way to write anonymous functions - filter orders, sort restaurants, process karo menu items functionally.
keyPoints: Syntax: (parameters) -> expression or (parameters) -> { statements }
Parentheses optional for single parameter
Curly braces required for multiple statements
return keyword use karte hain required for multiple statements
Can access final or effectively final variables
Works with functional interfaces sirf
Interview: What is lambda? Anonymous function that implements functional interface

concepts.name: Functional Interfaces
concepts.explanation: Interfaces with single abstract method - Predicate for filtering, Function for transformation, Consumer for process karoing.
keyPoints: Functional interface: interface with single abstract method (SAM)
@FunctionalInterface annotation (optional but recommended)
Predicate<T>: T → boolean (test)
Function<T,R>: T → R (apply)
Consumer<T>: T → void (accept)
Supplier<T>: () → T (get)
BiFunction<T,U,R>: (T,U) → R
Can have default and static methods
Interview: Common functional interfaces? Predicate, Function, Consumer, Supplier

concepts.name: Method References
concepts.explanation: Shorthand for lambda expressions - reference existing methods instead of writing lambda.
keyPoints: Shorthand for lambda expressions
Types: ClassName::staticMethod, object::instanceMethod, ClassName::instanceMethod, ClassName::new
More readable than lambda for simple cases
System.out::println same as x -> System.out.println(x)
String::toUpperCase same as s -> s.toUpperCase()
Order::new same as id -> new Order(id)
Interview: When to use method reference? When lambda just calls existing method

concepts.name: Stream Basics
concepts.explanation: Functional-style operations on collections - filter orders, totals calculate karo, transform data without loops.
keyPoints: Stream: sequence of elements supporting sequential and parallel operations
Streams are lazy - operations executed sirf when terminal operation called
Intermediate operations: filter, map, sorted (return Stream)
Terminal operations: collect, forEach, count (return result)
Streams can be used sirf once
Don't modify source during stream operations
Interview: Stream vs Collection? Stream for process karoing, Collection for storage

concepts.name: Stream Operations
concepts.explanation: Common stream operations - sorting, limiting, skip karoping, distinct, reducing data.
keyPoints: sorted() sorts in natural order, sorted(comparator) for custom
distinct() removes duplicates using equals()
limit(n) takes first n elements
skip karo(n) skip karos first n elements
min/max return Optional (may be empty)
reduce() combines elements into single result
findFirst() returns first match, findAny() for parallel streams
Interview: What is reduce? Combines stream elements using associative function

concepts.name: Collectors
concepts.explanation: Collect stream results into different forms - lists, sets, maps, grouping, partitioning.
keyPoints: Collectors.toList(), toSet(), toMap() for basic collections
groupingBy() groups elements by classifier function
partitioningBy() splits into two groups (true/false)
joining() concatenates strings
counting(), summingDouble(), averagingDouble() for aggregations
summarizingDouble() provides all statistics at once
Can combine collectors: groupingBy + counting
Interview: groupingBy vs partitioningBy? groupingBy for multiple groups, partitioningBy for two groups

concepts.name: Creating Threads
concepts.explanation: Run multiple tasks concurrently - process karo multiple orders simultaneously, handle karo multiple delivery partners, parallel restaurant updates.
keyPoints: Two ways: extend Thread or implement Runnable
Runnable preferred (allows extending other classes)
start() banta hai new thread and calls run()
Calling run() directly doesn't create new thread
Thread.sleep() pauses current thread
InterruptedException must be handle karod
Interview: Thread vs Runnable? Runnable more flexible, allows multiple inheritance

concepts.name: Thread Synchronization
concepts.explanation: Prevent race conditions when multiple threads access shared data - protect order counter, wallet balance, inventory.
keyPoints: synchronized prevents multiple threads from executing code simultaneously
synchronized method locks entire object
synchronized block locks specific object
Only one thread can execute synchronized code at a time
join() waits for thread to complete
Prevents race conditions but may cause performance overhead
Interview: What is race condition? Multiple threads accessing shared data causing inconsistent results

concepts.name: Executor Framework
concepts.explanation: Manage thread pools efficiently - handle karo multiple orders without create karna too many threads, reuse threads.
keyPoints: ExecutorService manages thread pool
newFixedThreadPool(n): fixed number of threads
newCachedThreadPool(): banta hai threads as needed
newSingleThreadExecutor(): single thread
submit() submits task for execution
shutdown() stops accepting new tasks
awaitTermination() waits for tasks to complete
Interview: Why use Executor? Better thread management, reusability, control

concepts.name: Concurrent Collections
concepts.explanation: Thread-safe collections - share data between threads safely without explicit synchronization.
keyPoints: ConcurrentHashMap: thread-safe HashMap, better than synchronized HashMap
CopyOnWriteArrayList: thread-safe ArrayList, good for read-heavy scenarios
BlockingQueue: thread-safe queue with blocking operations
No need for explicit synchronization
Better performance than synchronized collections
Interview: ConcurrentHashMap vs Hashtable? ConcurrentHashMap allows concurrent reads, Hashtable locks entire map

concepts.name: Reading and Writing Files
concepts.explanation: Read/write order data, save customer information, export reports, load configuration.
keyPoints: Use try-with-resources for automatic resource closing
FileWriter/FileReader for character streams
BufferedReader/BufferedWriter for efficient reading/writing
Files class (Java 7+) provides simpler API
Paths.get() banta hai Path object
Files.readAllLines() reads entire file into List
Files.write() writes List to file
Interview: Why try-with-resources? Automatically closes resources, prevents leaks

concepts.name: Serialization
concepts.explanation: Convert objects to bytes for storage/transmission - save Order objects, cache Customer data, send data over network.
keyPoints: Implement Serializable interface to make class serializable
serialVersionUID for version control
transient keyword use karte hain excludes field from serialization
ObjectOutputStream for serialization
ObjectInputStream for deserialization
All fields must be Serializable
static fields not serialized
Interview: What is serialization? Converting object to byte stream for storage/transmission

concepts.name: static Keyword
concepts.explanation: Belongs to class, not instance - shared counter for all orders, utility methods, constants.
keyPoints: static variable: shared by all instances
static method: called on class, not object
static block: initialization code, runs once
Cannot access instance members from static context
Can access static members from instance context
Interview: When to use static? Utility methods, constants, shared data

concepts.name: final Keyword
concepts.explanation: Prevents modification - constant values, prevent method overriding, prevent inheritance.
keyPoints: final variable: constant, reassign nahi kar sakte
final method: cannot be overridden by subclasses
final class: cannot be extended
final parameter: reassign nahi kar sakte in method
final with objects: reference cannot change, but object can be modified
Interview: Why make class final? Prevent inheritance, ensure immutability

concepts.name: this and super Keywords
concepts.explanation: Reference current and parent objects - distinguish parameters from fields, call parent constructors/methods.
keyPoints: this: refers to current object
this.field: access instance variable
this(): call another constructor in same class
super: refers to parent class
super.method(): call parent method
super(): call parent constructor (must be first line)
Interview: When to use this? Distinguish parameter from field, return current object

concepts.name: LocalDate, LocalTime, LocalDateTime
concepts.explanation: Modern date/time API - track order timestamps, delivery schedules, restaurant opening hours.
keyPoints: LocalDate: date without time (2024-01-15)
LocalTime: time without date (14:30:45)
LocalDateTime: date + time (2024-01-15T14:30:45)
Immutable: operations return new objects
now() gets current date/time
of() banta hai specific date/time
parse() converts String to date/time
format() converts date/time to String
plus/minus methods for arithmetic
Interview: Why use new API over Date? Immutable, thread-safe, clearer API

concepts.name: Duration and Period
concepts.explanation: Measure time differences - calculate delivery time, order age, subscription duration.
keyPoints: Duration: time-based amount (hours, minutes, seconds)
Period: date-based amount (years, months, days)
between() calculates difference
to*() methods convert to specific units
of*() methods create specific durations/periods
Use Duration for time, Period for dates
Interview: Duration vs Period? Duration for time, Period for dates

concepts.name: Wrapper Classes & Autoboxing
concepts.explanation: Object wrappers for primitives - store order counts in collections, handle karo null values, use utility methods.
keyPoints: Wrapper classes: Integer, Double, Boolean, Character, etc.
Autoboxing: automatic primitive → wrapper conversion
Unboxing: automatic wrapper → primitive conversion
parseInt(), parseDouble() convert String to primitive
toString(), valueOf() convert to String
Wrappers allow null values (primitives cannot)
Use wrappers in collections (ArrayList<Integer>)
Interview: Why wrappers? Collections need objects, not primitives

concepts.name: Optional for Null Safety
concepts.explanation: Avoid NullPointerException - handle karo missing customer data, optional delivery instructions, nullable restaurant info.
keyPoints: Optional: container that may or may not contain value
of(): banta hai Optional with non-null value
empty(): banta hai empty Optional
ofNullable(): safe with null values
isPresent(): check karo ki value exists
get(): retrieve value (throws if empty)
orElse(): provide default value
orElseGet(): provide default via supplier
orElseThrow(): throw exception if empty
map(): transform value if present
filter(): keep value if matches condition
Interview: Why Optional? Explicit handling of absence, avoid NullPointerException

concepts.name: Math Class
concepts.explanation: Mathematical operations - calculate distances, round prices, generate order IDs, compute ratings.
concepts.code:
int max = Math.max(100, 200);  // 200
int min = Math.min(100, 200);  // 100
int abs = Math.abs(-50);       // 50

double pow = Math.pow(5, 2);   // 25.0
double sqrt = Math.sqrt(25);   // 5.0

double price = 299.567;
long rounded = Math.round(price);  // 300
double ceil = Math.ceil(price);    // 300.0
double floor = Math.floor(price);  // 299.0
keyPoints: Math.max(), Math.min(): find maximum/minimum
Math.abs(): absolute value
Math.pow(): power (x^y)
Math.sqrt(): square root
Math.round(): round to nearest integer
Math.ceil(): round up
Math.floor(): round down
Math.random(): random double (0.0 to 1.0)
All methods are static
Interview: How to generate random int in range? (int)(Math.random() * range) + min

concepts.name: Random Class
concepts.explanation: Better random number generation - generate order IDs, select random restaurants, create test data.
keyPoints: Random class: better than Math.random()
nextInt(): random integer
nextInt(bound): random int from 0 to bound-1
nextDouble(): random double (0.0 to 1.0)
nextBoolean(): random boolean
Can set seed for reproducible sequences
Thread-safe alternative: ThreadLocalRandom
Interview: Random vs Math.random()? Random class more flexible, can set seed

concepts.name: var Keyword (Java 10+)
concepts.explanation: Type inference for local variables - cleaner code, less repetition, compiler infers type.
keyPoints: var: local variable type inference (Java 10+)
Compiler infers type from right-hand side
Only for local variables (not fields, parameters, returns)
Must be initialized when declared
Cannot infer from null
Improves readability for complex types
Type is still static (not dynamic like JavaScript)
Interview: Is var dynamic typing? No, still static - type inferred at compile time

concepts.name: Text Blocks (Java 15+)
concepts.explanation: Multi-line strings - JSON responses, SQL queries, HTML templates, formatted receipts.
keyPoints: Text blocks: multi-line strings with """ (Java 15+)
No need to escape quotes or add \n
Preserves formatting and indentation
Trailing whitespace removed automatically
Use formatted() for string interpolation
Great for JSON, SQL, HTML, formatted text
Interview: Why text blocks? Cleaner multi-line strings, better readability

concepts.name: Switch Expressions (Java 14+)
concepts.explanation: Enhanced switch with return values - cleaner code, break ki zarurat nahi, pattern matching.
keyPoints: Switch expressions return values (Java 14+)
Use -> instead of : (break ki zarurat nahi)
Can assign result to variable
Multiple cases: case 1, 2, 3 ->
Use yield for code blocks
Must be exhaustive (cover all cases or have default)
Can throw exceptions
Interview: Switch expression vs statement? Expression returns value, break ki zarurat nahi

concepts.name: Records (Java 16+)
concepts.explanation: Immutable data carriers - DTOs for orders, customer info, menu items. Auto-generates constructor, getters, equals, hashCode, toString.
keyPoints: Records: immutable data carriers (Java 16+)
Auto-generates: constructor, getters, equals, hashCode, toString
Syntax: record Name(Type field1, Type field2, ...) {}
Fields are final and private
Getters have same name as fields (no 'get' prefix)
Can add custom methods
Compact constructor for validation
Perfect for DTOs, value objects
Cannot extend other classes (but can implement interfaces)
Interview: When to use records? Immutable data carriers, DTOs, value objects

concepts.name: Sealed Classes (Java 17+)
concepts.explanation: Restrict inheritance - define allowed subclasses for order status, payment methods, user types.
keyPoints: Sealed classes: restrict which classes can extend (Java 17+)
Syntax: sealed class Name permits Class1, Class2, ...
Permitted subclasses must be: final, sealed, or non-sealed
All permitted classes must be in same module/package
Enables exhaustive pattern matching in switch
Compiler knows all possible subtypes
Use for: closed hierarchies, state machines, type-safe enums
Interview: Why sealed classes? Control inheritance, exhaustive pattern matching
